commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human maintainer/security review needs to approve the arbitrary host bind-mount boundary; automation should not choose that policy.; Needs attention: Needs maintainer/security attention because the diff adds explicit arbitrary host bind mounts to Docker-run local-container leases.; Review finding: [P3] Leave the changelog entry to release owners — CHANGELOG.md:7 (sha=4099c42a49346edf064c4eac3691fb8698fd146d)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a repeatable CLI-only `--local-container-volume host:container[:ro]` flag for local-container runs, forwards values to Docker `-v`, and adds docs/tests for the config exclusion.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. The PR body does include terminal proof that the new flag mounted a host directory in a real local-container run.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer/security review needs to approve the arbitrary host bind-mount boundary; automation should not choose that policy.

target.title: feat(local-container): add --local-container-volume for host bind mounts
target.body:
## Summary

- Add repeatable `--local-container-volume host:container[:ro]` flag
- Passes through to Docker `-v` in `createContainer()`
- CLI-only — intentionally NOT loaded from repo-local `.crabbox.yaml` (security: untrusted checkouts must not request host mounts)

## Motivation

When using `--provider local-container`, there is no way to mount host directories into the container. This blocks use cases like:

- Mounting credential directories so a containerized service can authenticate
- Sharing a model cache to avoid re-downloading
- Mounting SSL certificates or SSH keys for integration tests

```sh
crabbox run --provider local-container \
  --local-container-volume ~/.config/myapp:/home/crabbox/.config/myapp:ro \
  --local-container-volume /var/cache/models:/cache:ro \
  -- myapp serve --port 8080
```

## Proof

```
$ crabbox run --provider local-container \
    --local-container-volume ~/.openclaw:/home/crabbox/.openclaw:ro \
    --no-sync -- ls -la /home/crabbox/.openclaw/

provisioning provider=local-container lease=cbx_792c115e56cb slug=amber-barnacle
running on 127.0.0.1 ls -la /home/crabbox/.openclaw/
-rwxrwxrwx 1 root root 13660 Jun  3 15:41 /home/crabbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json
drwxrwxrwx 1 root root 4096  Jun  2 23:09 /home/crabbox/.openclaw/credentials/auth-profiles
=== PROOF: Host directory mounted successfully ===
command complete in 79ms
```

Tested on Windows (Docker Desktop via WSL2). Docker's `-v` handles path translation natively on macOS (Docker Desktop/OrbStack), Linux (native), and Windows (Docker Desktop).

## Security

`localContainer.volumes` is **intentionally NOT loaded from `.crabbox.yaml`**. Bind mounts expose host paths and must be an explicit CLI action. A comment in `config.go` documents this decision. This matches the trust model of `--local-container-docker-socket`.

## Tests

- `TestCreateContainerMountsHostVolumes`: verifies repeated volumes produce `-v` args in Docker run
- `TestCreateContainerNoVolumesWhenEmpty`: verifies no spurious mounts when Volumes is empty
- `TestVolumeListFlagParsesRepeated`: verifies the flag type appends correctly for repeated `--local-container-volume` usage

## Changes

- `internal/cli/config.go`: Add `Volumes []string` to `LocalContainerConfig`; explicit comment blocking YAML config loading
- `internal/providers/localcontainer/flags.go`: Add `volumeListFlag` type (pointer-based for correct `flag.Var` semantics) and `--local-container-volume` flag
- `internal/providers/localcontainer/backend.go`: Append `-v` flags for each volume in `createContainer()`
- `internal/providers/localcontainer/backend_test.go`: 3 focused tests

Closes #201
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
